---
title: "Larvae experiment script"
author: "Ruben Martinez"
date: "2025-10-08"
output: html_document
---
All of the mentioned commands were stored in .sbatch files, and ran with the following settings:
```{bash}
#!/bin/bash
#
#SBATCH --ntasks=1
#SBATCH -J whatever_the_pipeline
#SBATCH -o o_%x_%J.txt
#SBATCH -e e_%x_%J.txt
#SBATCH -p gpu_p
#SBATCH -q gpu_normal
#SBATCH --cpus-per-task=20
#SBATCH --gres=gpu:2
#SBATCH --mem=200G
#SBATCH -t 24:00:00
#SBATCH --mail-type=ALL
#SBATCH --mail-user=ruben.martinezcuesta@helmholtz-munich.de

source /home/comi/ruben.martinez/tools/apps/mamba/etc/profile.d/conda.sh
```

Basecalling with Dorado
```{bash}
#Running Dorado stored in a common directory
/lustre/groups/comi/tools/dorado-0.9.5-linux-x64/bin/dorado basecaller hac /project/genomics/Nanopore_data/2025_01_22_P2Blik_Ruben_1/greenroof_dataset/pod5 --kit-name SQK-RBK114-24 \
--no-trim \
--device cuda:0,1 > /project/genomics/Nanopore_data/2025_01_22_P2Blik_Ruben_1/gr_pool_0425.bam
```

Demultiplexing with Dorado
```{bash}
/lustre/groups/comi/tools/dorado-0.9.5-linux-x64/bin/dorado demux /project/genomics/Nanopore_data/2025_01_22_P2Blik_Ruben_1/gr_pool_0425.bam \
--output-dir gr_pool_demux_0425 \
--no-classify \
--emit-fastq
```

Trimming of ONT adapters with Porechop
```{bash}
#With Porechop installed as a conda env
source /lustre/groups/comi/tools/miniconda3/etc/profile.d/conda.sh

conda activate porechop

for file in /project/genomics/Nanopore_data/2025_01_22_P2Blik_Ruben_1/gr_pool_demux_0425\*.fastq; do
  # Extract the base name of the file (without .fastq extension)
  base_name="${file%.fastq}"
  
  # Run Porechop on the file and output to a new file with _trimmed suffix
  porechop -i "$file" -o "${base_name}_trimmed.fastq" -v 1
  
done

mv *_trimmed.fastq /project/genomics/Nanopore_data/2025_01_22_P2Blik_Ruben_1/gr_pool_demux_0425\trimmed
```

Quality filtering with Chopper
```{bash}
#With chopper installed in a conda env
source /lustre/groups/comi/tools/miniconda3/etc/profile.d/conda.sh

conda activate chopper

for file in /project/genomics/Nanopore_data/2025_01_22_P2Blik_Ruben_1/gr_pool_demux_0425\trimmed/*.fastq; do
  #Extract the base name of the file (without .fastq extension)
 base_name=${file%.fastq}

chopper --quality 10 --minlength 500 --threads 10 -i $file > ${base_name}_filt.fastq

done

mv *_filt.fastq /project/genomics/Nanopore_data/2025_01_22_P2Blik_Ruben_1/gr_pool_demux_0425\trimmed/filtered

```

Remove host (E.danica; Edan2.0 assembly from NCBI; GCA_000507165.2) from samples with minimap2
```{bash}
source /lustre/groups/comi/tools/miniconda3/etc/profile.d/conda.sh

conda activate minimap2

minimap2 -d e_danica_index.mmi GCA_000507165.2_Edan_2.0_genomic.fna

for file in *_filt.fastq.fastq; do
  # Extract the base name of the file (without .fastq.gz extension)
  base_name="${file%trimmed_filt.fastq}"
  
  # Run minimap2 and output to a SAM file
  minimap2 -a -x map-ont -t 4 e_danica_index.mmi "$file" > "${base_name}.sam" 
done
```

Filtering out host reads with samtools
```{bash}
source /lustre/groups/comi/tools/miniconda3/etc/profile.d/conda.sh

conda activate minimap2
#samtools was also installed in this conda env
#conda install samtools -c bioconda

for s in *.sam; do
    base=$(basename "$s" .sam)
    samtools view -Sb "$s" | samtools sort -n -o "${base}.bam"
    samtools fastq -f 4 "${base}.bam" > "${base}_wh.fastq"
done
```

Assembly with metaMDBG
```{bash}
source /lustre/groups/comi/tools/miniconda3/etc/profile.d/conda.sh

conda activate metamdbg

metaMDBG asm --out-dir metamdbg_OTL1_T1 \
--threads 10 \
--in-ont SQK-RBK114-24_barcode01_wh.fastq SQK-RBK114-24_barcode02_wh.fastq SQK-RBK114-24_barcode03_wh.fastq SQK-RBK114-24_barcode04_wh.fastq SQK-RBK114-24_barcode05_wh.fastq SQK-RBK114-24_barcode17_wh.fastq
```

Polishing the metaMDBG assembly with racon
```{bash}
#We need to map the reads to the assembly as a pre-step
#Number of racon rounds depending on the quality of the assembly --> check with metaQUAST
srun -p cpu_p -q cpu_normal \
     --job-name=minimap2 \
     --cpus-per-task=8 \
     --mem=32G \
     --time=06:00:00 \
bash -c "minimap2 -ax map-ont metamdbg_OTL1_T1/final.contigs.fa reads_OTL1_T1.fastq > map_assembly_OTL1_T1.sam"

srun -p cpu_p -q cpu_normal \
     --job-name=racon \
     --cpus-per-task=8 \
     --mem=32G \
     --time=06:00:00 \
bash -c "racon -t 14 -m 8 -x -6 -g -8 -w 500 reads_OTL1_T1.fastq map_assembly_OTL1_T1.sam metamdbg_OTL1_T1/final.contigs.fa > polished_assembly_OTL1_T1_1.fa"
```

Checking the quality of the assembly with MetaQUAST
```{bash}
srun -p cpu_p -q cpu_normal \
     --job-name=metaquast \
     --cpus-per-task=8 \
     --mem=32G \
     --time=06:00:00 \
bash -c "metaquast -t 8 -o quast --max-ref-number 0 final.contigs.fa"
```

```{bash}
srun -p cpu_p -q cpu_normal \
  --job-name=minimap2 \
  --cpus-per-task=8 \
  --mem=60G \
  --time=06:00:00 \
  bash -c "minimap2 -t 8 -ax map-ont megahit_OTL1_T1/polished_assembly_OTL1_T1_2.fa reads_OTL1_ST1.fastq \
    | samtools view -F 3584 -b \
    | samtools sort -@ 8 -o map_assembly_racon_OTL1_T1.sorted.bam"
```

Binning with LRBinner
```{bash}
python /home/comi/ruben.martinez/LRBinner/lrbinner.py contigs \
--reads-path reads_OTL1_T1.fastq \
--k-size 3 \
--contigs polished_assembly_OTL1_T1_1.fa \
--threads 15 \
--output lrbinner_OTL1_T1 \
--seed 1234 \ 
#for reproducibility
-sep #to split the bins
```

Binning with VAMB
```{bash}
vamb bin default --outdir vamb_OTL1_T1 \
--fasta polished_assembly_OTL1_T1_1.fa \
--bamdir map_assembly_OTL1 \
-m 1000 -o _ \
--minfasta 20000 \
--seed 123
```

Binning with MetaWrap
```{bash}
metawrap binning -a polished_assembly_OTL1_T1_1.fa \
--metabat2 --maxbin2 --concoct \
-o metawrap_OTL1_T1 \
--single-end SQK-RBK114-24_barcode01_wh.fastq SQK-RBK114-24_barcode02_wh.fastq SQK-RBK114-24_barcode03_wh.fastq SQK-RBK114-24_barcode04_wh.fastq SQK-RBK114-24_barcode05_wh.fastq SQK-RBK114-24_barcode17_wh.fastq
```

Purifying the bins with MAGpurify using the output of MetaWrap
```{bash}
# Input/output paths
INPUT_DIR="metawrap_OTL1_T1/metabat2_bins"
OUT_BASE="metawrap_OTL1_T1/metabat2_bins_magpurify"
export MAGPURIFYDB=/lustre/groups/comi/tools/databases/MAGpurify-db-v1.0

mkdir -p "$OUT_BASE"

# Number of bins to process in parallel
THREADS=10  # change this based on your CPU cores

# Function that runs MAGpurify for one bin
run_magpurify_for_bin() {
    bin_path="$1"
    bin_name=$(basename "$bin_path" .fa)
    outdir="${OUT_BASE}/${bin_name}_magpurify"

    echo "===== Running MAGpurify for ${bin_name} ====="
    mkdir -p "$outdir"

    magpurify phylo-markers "$bin_path" "$outdir"
    magpurify clade-markers "$bin_path" "$outdir"
    magpurify tetra-freq "$bin_path" "$outdir"
    magpurify gc-content "$bin_path" "$outdir"
    magpurify coverage "$bin_path" "$outdir" map_assembly_racon_OTL1_T1.sorted.bam
    magpurify known-contam "$bin_path" "$outdir"

    # Clean bin
    magpurify clean-bin "$bin_path" "$outdir" "${outdir}/${bin_name}.clean.fa"
}

export -f run_magpurify_for_bin
export OUT_BASE

# Run in parallel for all .fa bins
ls "$INPUT_DIR"/*.fa | parallel -j "$THREADS" run_magpurify_for_bin {}
```

```{bash}
find . -name "*.clean.fa" -exec mv {} ../concoct_bins/ \;
find . -name "*.clean.fa" -exec mv {} ../metabat2_bins/ \;
find . -name "*.clean.fa" -exec mv {} ../maxbin2_bins/ \;
```

Check bins' quality with Checkm2
```{bash}
checkm2 predict \
--input metawrap_OTL1_T1 -x .fa \
--threads 10 \
--output-directory metawrap_OTL1_T1/checkm2 \
--database_path /lustre/groups/comi/tools/databases/CheckM2_db/uniref100.KO.1.dmnd
```

Annotating the bins with DRAM
```{bash}
source /lustre/groups/comi/tools/miniconda3/etc/profile.d/conda.sh

conda activate DRAM

DRAM.py annotate \
  -i 'metawrap_OTL1_T1/*.fa' \
  -o metawrap_OTL1_T1/DRAM \
  --min_contig_size 200 \
  --threads 10 \
  --use_vogdb
```
